expectations.text: hook_views_data() includes 'group' key with a human-readable label on the table definition (missing group = 'Broken handler' error in Views UI, and fields become unfindable among hundreds of ungrouped entries)
expectations.evidence: resource_directory.module line 16: 'group' => t('Resource Links') is set in $data['resource_links']['table']. Confirmed at runtime: ddev drush php-eval returned 'Table group: Resource Links'.

expectations.text: hook_views_data() is used for the custom table (NOT hook_views_data_alter() which is for modifying existing tables from other modules -- data_alter on your own table is an anti-pattern)
expectations.evidence: resource_directory.module implements only resource_directory_views_data() (line 11). Grep for 'views_data_alter' across all module files returns no matches.

expectations.text: Custom filter plugin extends FilterPluginBase or InOperator (NOT a generic PluginBase class -- ViewsFilter plugins require filter-specific methods like query() and valueForm())
expectations.evidence: src/Plugin/views/filter/CategoryFilter.php line 12: 'class CategoryFilter extends InOperator' with use statement 'use Drupal\views\Plugin\views\filter\InOperator' at line 7.

expectations.text: Filter plugin file is in the correct PSR-4 path: src/Plugin/views/filter/ with lowercase 'views' (NOT src/Plugin/Views/Filter/ with capital V -- Drupal Views plugin manager scans lowercase 'views' namespace specifically)
expectations.evidence: File exists at /tmp/d10-views-dev-without/web/modules/custom/resource_directory/src/Plugin/views/filter/CategoryFilter.php — all lowercase path confirmed by ls output. Plugin is registered at runtime: ddev drush php-eval confirmed 'resource_directory_category plugin: FOUND'.

expectations.text: hook_views_data() table definition includes 'base' key making the table available as a Views base table for creating new views (not just individual field definitions without a base table entry)
expectations.evidence: resource_directory.module lines 17-21: 'base' => ['field' => 'id', 'title' => t('Resource Links'), 'help' => ...] is set in $data['resource_links']['table']. Runtime confirms: ddev drush php-eval returned 'base key: present' and 'resource_links as base table: Resource Links'.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en resource_directory returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: cd /tmp/d10-views-dev-without && ddev drush en resource_directory -y returned '[notice] Already installed: resource_directory' with EXIT:0.

expectations.text: Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to /admin/structure/views/add -- verify the resource_links table appears as an available base table option (via eval-browser)
expectations.evidence: No eval-browser report available; verified via drush. ddev drush php-eval confirmed resource_links is registered with a 'base' key and appears in all base tables list (11 total). Plugin registered successfully, module enabled, views_ui enabled. resource_links title 'Resource Links' would appear in the Views add form base table selector.

expectations.text: Navigate to /admin/structure/views -- verify the Views listing page loads without 'Broken handler' errors related to the resource_directory module (via eval-browser)
expectations.evidence: No eval-browser report available; verified via drush. The category filter plugin 'resource_directory_category' is registered successfully (confirmed by plugin.manager.views.filter). No watchdog entries for views errors. The filter id in views_data matches the registered plugin id, so no broken handler condition exists.

expectations.text: Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
expectations.evidence: phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice --extensions=php,module,install,yml returned EXIT:1 with 2 errors in resource_directory.install: line 64 'Empty installation hooks are not necessary' and line 71 'Empty installation hooks are not necessary' (the hook_install() and hook_uninstall() functions contain only comments and are empty per Drupal standards).
